heading: Grants to promote childhood nutrition and physical activity
label: 280h Grants to promote childhood nutrition and physical activity
notes: CodificationAnother section 399W of act , was renumbered section 399V–1 and is classified to .July 1, 1944section 280g–12 of this title

children.heading: In general
children.label: a In general
children.text: The Secretary, acting though the Director of the Centers for Disease Control and Prevention, shall award competitive grants to States and political subdivisions of States for the development and implementation of State and community-based intervention programs to promote good nutrition and physical activity in children and adolescents.

how the applicant proposes to develop a comprehensive program of school- and community-based approaches to encourage and promote good nutrition and appropriate levels of physical activity with respect to children or adolescents in local communities;

the manner in which the applicant shall coordinate with appropriate State and local authorities, such as State and local school departments, State departments of health, chronic disease directors, State directors of programs under , 5-a-day coordinators, governors councils for physical activity and good nutrition, and State and local parks and recreation departments; andsection 1786 of this title
the manner in which the applicant will evaluate the effectiveness of the program carried out under this section.
develop, implement, disseminate, and evaluate school- and community-based strategies in States to reduce inactivity and improve dietary choices among children and adolescents;
expand opportunities for physical activity programs in school- and community-based settings; and
develop, implement, and evaluate programs that promote good eating habits and physical activity including opportunities for children with cognitive and physical disabilities.
provide States and political subdivisions of States with technical support in the development and implementation of programs under this section; and
disseminate information about effective strategies and interventions in preventing and treating obesity through the promotion of good nutrition and physical activity.

children.heading: Limitation on administrative costs
children.label: e Limitation on administrative costs
children.text: Not to exceed 10 percent of the amount of a grant awarded to the State or political subdivision under subsection (a) for a fiscal year may be used by the State or political subdivision for administrative expenses.

children.heading: Term
children.label: f Term
children.text: A grant awarded under subsection (a) shall be for a term of 3 years.

children.heading: Definition
children.label: g Definition
children.text: In this section, the term “children and adolescents” means individuals who do not exceed 18 years of age.

children.heading: Authorization of appropriations
children.label: h Authorization of appropriations
children.text: There are authorized to be appropriated to carry out this section such sums as may be necessary for each of the fiscal years 2001 through 2005.
